solution.p: We will factor y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2} where a=1, b=7, and c=-8.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.

For y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2}, since the product of 8 and -1 is -8, and the sum of 8 and -1 is 7, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2}\amp =\left(y+8t\right)\mathopen{}\left(y-t\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (-8) = -8.

The number -8 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-8)\amp -1(8)\amp 2(-4)\amp -2(4)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 7, and this sum happens with (-1)+8=7 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2}\amp =y^{2}-yt+8yt-8t^{2}
ewline \amp =y\mathopen{}\left(y-tight)+8t\mathopen{}\left(y-tight)
ewline \amp =\left(y+8tight)\mathopen{}\left(y-tight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2} into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}
The third term's coefficient, -8, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-8)\amp -1(8)\amp 2(-4)\amp -2(4)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2} by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}+7yt-8t^{2}\amp =\left(y+8t\right)\mathopen{}\left(y-t\right)\end{aligned}
